name: azd-deployment
description: Deploy containerized applications to Azure Container Apps using Azure Developer CLI (azd). Use when setting up azd projects, writing azure.yaml configuration, creating Bicep infrastructure for Container Apps, configuring remote builds with ACR, implementing idempotent deployments, managing environment variables across local/.azure/Bicep, or troubleshooting azd up failures. Triggers on requests for azd configuration, Container Apps deployment, multi-service deployments, and infrastructure-as-code with Bicep.
capabilities: Configure azure.yaml for multi-service container apps deployment
Create Bicep infrastructure templates for Azure Container Apps
Set up environment variable flow between azd and Bicep outputs
Implement pre and post deployment hooks for RBAC and custom domains
Configure managed identity for Azure services like OpenAI and AI Search
Use remote builds for cross-platform compatibility
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is legitimate DevOps documentation for Azure Container Apps deployment. All detected patterns (external commands, network, filesystem) are standard Azure CLI operations used in deployment workflows. The heuristic alerts about credential access and command execution are false positives - the skill contains only documentation and example configurations, not actual credential handling or malicious code.
valueStatement: This skill provides documentation and reference examples for deploying containerized applications to Azure Container Apps using Azure Developer CLI (azd) and Bicep infrastructure templates.
userTitle: Deploy to Azure Container Apps with azd
limitations: This is a documentation skill only - it provides reference examples but does not execute deployments
Requires Azure subscription and azd CLI to be installed
Does not include actual infrastructure code - only patterns and examples
ARM/M1 Mac users must use remoteBuild to avoid architecture mismatch

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to Azure Container Apps
useCases.title: Set up new azd project
useCases.description: Initialize a new Azure Developer CLI project with proper azure.yaml configuration for container apps deployment

useCases.title: Configure Bicep infrastructure
useCases.description: Create reusable Bicep modules for container apps environment, with outputs that auto-populate environment variables

useCases.targetUser: Platform engineers setting up deployment pipelines
useCases.title: Implement CI/CD hooks
useCases.description: Add preprovision, postprovision, and postdeploy hooks for RBAC assignments and custom domain handling

promptTemplates.title: Basic azd setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to initialize a new azd project for deploying to Azure Container Apps with a Python backend.

promptTemplates.title: Configure azure.yaml
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an azure.yaml configuration for a TypeScript frontend and Python backend deployed to container apps with remote builds enabled.

promptTemplates.title: Bicep outputs to env vars
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I configure Bicep outputs so they automatically populate environment variables in the .azure folder?

promptTemplates.title: Managed identity RBAC
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to assign RBAC roles to a container app managed identity for Azure OpenAI and AI Search access using postprovision hooks.

bestPractices: Always use remoteBuild: true in azure.yaml to avoid AMD64/M1 architecture mismatch
Use azd env set for secrets instead of hardcoding in main.parameters.json
Add || true to RBAC hook commands to prevent failures when roles already exist
antiPatterns: Using local build instead of remoteBuild will fail on ARM Macs deploying to AMD64 Azure
Manually editing .azure/<env>/.env files - these are auto-populated by Bicep outputs
Skipping the resourceToken in internal DNS names - required for unique service discovery

faq.question: What is Azure Developer CLI (azd)?
faq.answer: Azure Developer CLI (azd) is an open-source tool that provides a developer-friendly way to provision and deploy Azure applications. It uses azure.yaml for configuration and Bicep for infrastructure.

faq.question: Why use remoteBuild instead of local builds?
faq.answer: RemoteBuild builds your Docker images in Azure Container Registry. This avoids architecture mismatch issues when developing on ARM Macs (M1/M2) and deploying to AMD64 Azure Container Apps.

faq.question: How do environment variables flow from Bicep to my app?
faq.answer: Bicep output statements auto-populate the .azure/<env>/.env file. Define outputs like 'output SERVICE_BACKEND_URI string = backend.outputs.uri' in your Bicep code.

faq.question: Can I preserve custom domains added via Azure Portal?
faq.answer: Yes, use preprovision hooks to save customDomains before provisioning, then restore them in postprovision. Set customDomains to null in Bicep to preserve Portal-added domains.

faq.question: How do I assign RBAC roles to my container app?
faq.answer: Enable SystemAssigned identity in your Bicep, capture the principalId in outputs, then use postprovision hooks with 'az role assignment create' commands.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This is documentation-only - it provides reference examples for Azure deployment patterns. No actual credentials or deployment operations are performed.
